input.command:
gh pr create --title "test: backend coverage audit — close the four sub-80% modules + enforce --cov-fail-under=80" --milestone "Week 8 — Unit testing & coverage (≥80%)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- **The Week-8 exit-gate deliverable, backend half.** Closes the last four modules below the 80% bar found by the coverage audit, then flips `--cov-fail-under=0 → 80` in `pyproject.toml`.
- Gap tests (25 new): `alerting/email.py` **55→99%** (publish over a captured fake SMTP: STARTTLS/login/send order, recipients, threshold/disabled/missing-secret gating, text-body truncation + falsy-zero count note), `alerting/slack.py` **74→100%** (happy-path POST payload + `raise_for_status`, SSRF host-allowlist block, gating, truncation), `datasources/flatfile.py` **74→100%** (`download_bytes`/`list_files` S3+ADLS dispatch, `FileRef` mapping incl. missing mtime, `finally`-close discipline, real client construction with fail-fast timeouts), `core/auth.py` **77→97%** (claim fallback chain `preferred_username→email→upn→""`, `init_auth` real-mode load + fail-closed raise, real-mode upsert from claims, unconfigured 503).
- Backend totals after: **98.3% lines, 1233 tests** (was 96%, 1208). The two remaining uncovered `auth.py` lines are the import-time mode-select branch, unreachable without a module reload under Azure env.

## Linked issue

Refs #177 (W8 epic) — the CI-gate roadmap row (backend half)

## Type of change

- [x] test — test-only change

## Checklist

- [x] **Manually tested locally** — full suite `pytest backend/tests/` 1233/1233 green with the gate enforced ("Required test coverage of 80% reached. Total coverage: 98.30%")
- [x] **Single functionality** — the backend coverage-audit close + its gate
- [x] **Conventional commit title**
- [x] **No secrets, credentials, or `.env` files committed**
- [x] **Black + Ruff + mypy pass locally** (mypy incl. `backend/tests`)
- [ ] Prettier + ESLint — N/A
- [x] **Tests added/updated**
- [ ] Docs / ADRs — N/A
- [ ] **`docs/progress.md` updated** — deferred to the W8 close-out PR (the CI-gate row flips ✅ only once the frontend half lands too)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

result.output: https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/557

input.prompt:
Code-review finder (correctness): review branch chore/w8-backend-coverage-gaps vs main in /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ (`git diff main...HEAD`). Changes: new tests appended to backend/tests/alerting/test_slack_email_publishers.py, backend/tests/core/test_auth.py, backend/tests/datasources/test_flatfile.py, plus pyproject.toml flipping --cov-fail-under to 80.

Read the changed test files and the modules under test (backend/app/alerting/{email,slack}.py, backend/app/core/auth.py, backend/app/datasources/flatfile.py). Hunt: assertions that don't match real behavior, monkeypatch targets that miss (string-path patches of smtplib.SMTP / notification_service.get_config — do they patch the object the code under test actually calls?), cross-test pollution (module-level _FakeSmtp.instances ClassVar, monkeypatching the shared notification_service module), fake-SMTP semantics vs real smtplib context manager (e.g. __exit__ 'closed' ordering), auth tests mutating module globals (auth_mod.azure_scheme/_settings) without restore (monkeypatch handles it?), and whether test_init_auth_fails_closed constructs Settings correctly (env vars from the test env may leak in — Settings reads env). Also: does the gate flip risk failing CI runs that legitimately skip DB tests (CI always sets TEST_DATABASE_URL, but check the CI workflow's pytest invocation)?

Return up to 6 candidates as JSON (file, line, summary, failure_scenario) — only ones with a nameable failure scenario. Verify each against the code before including (state CONFIRMED/PLAUSIBLE). If none, [].

Code-review finder (reuse/simplification/efficiency + CLAUDE.md conventions): review branch chore/w8-backend-coverage-gaps vs main in /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ (`git diff main...HEAD`). Changes: new tests in backend/tests/alerting/test_slack_email_publishers.py, backend/tests/core/test_auth.py, backend/tests/datasources/test_flatfile.py + pyproject.toml gate flip.

Check for: duplicated helpers that existing test files already provide (grep backend/tests for similar fakes/stubs — e.g. an existing fake SMTP, fake secret store elsewhere to reuse, existing _report builders), copy-paste variation between the slack and email gating tests that could be table-driven, dead/vacuous assertions. Conventions: read /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/CLAUDE.md + CONTRIBUTING.md rule 4a (test failure modes, don't mock the seam under test, adversarial battery for data-ingesting code) — check the new tests comply; quote rule + line for any violation.

Return up to 6 candidates as JSON (file, line, summary, failure_scenario = concrete cost or quoted rule). If none, [].

input.command:
git -C /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ commit -m "test: honest frontend coverage — count all of src, close the 7 uninstrumented files, enforce the 80% line gate

Coverage previously counted only files the tests imported: 7 src files
(App shell, both auth providers, api/admin+me wrappers, the Monaco field)
were invisible to the number and a new untested page would never lower it.
Now coverage.include=['src'] counts everything (main.tsx excluded — the DOM
bootstrap can't mount under the runner), 22 new tests cover the seven files
(shell nav + admin gating + 404 + user menu; OIDC event subscribe/unsubscribe
+ silent-renew clear; per-mode current-user derivation + claim fallbacks;
table-driven admin/me wrappers; Monaco controlled-field glue), the vitest
lines threshold flips 0→80, and CI's frontend job runs pnpm test:coverage so
the gate actually blocks merges. Baseline at flip: 86.6% lines, all-src.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && git -C /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ push -u origin chore/w8-frontend-coverage-gate 2>&1 | tail -1
